mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns exceed 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 5.0 for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate robust demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces equity markets higher as corporate revenue growth accelerates over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening economic momentum that triggers short-term momentum buying in equities, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative returns during recovery phases, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect increased construction activity and consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST growth consistently precedes negative equity returns, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, inflationary pressures trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold, or the signal fails to predict returns during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals economic contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns dominate during GDP contraction periods, or the signal shows no predictive power when GDPC1 growth turns negative.
